facade_description: 594 Dundas Street West presents a commercial street frontage within the Kensington Market HCD. The street frontage spans roughly 6 metres across 2 storeys beneath a flat roof with a parapet, consistent with its commercial typology. The frontage is defined primarily by brick above a commercial storefront in a warm brick and storefront glazing palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 2 upper-window bays, using commercial plate-glass openings at ground level and double-hung sash windows above, and 1 principal entrance. Key storefront and facade features include brick cladding, decorative brickwork and stonework, flat-headed and segmental-arched window and door openings, leaded glass transoms, ornamented cornice, chamfered corner. HCD note: Rare purpose-built Victorian commercial structure at gateway to Augusta Avenue. Associated with George Eakin Gibbard, founder of Canadian Pharmaceutical Association.
heritage_summary: A rare purpose-built Victorian commercial building at the gateway to Augusta Avenue. Associated with George Eakin Gibbard, founder of Canadian Pharmaceutical Association.
